title: hymenogyne glabra subsp. glabra
intro: Hymenogyne glabra subsp. glabra, also known as Wolf's Bane or Northern Wolf's Bane, is a perennial plant species in the buttercup family, Ranunculaceae. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral effects. The health benefits of Hymenogyne glabra subsp. glabra include its potential use in treating various conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and skin irritations, as well as its ability to act as a natural insect repellent. Herbal preparations of this plant can be found in the form of infusions, tinctures, and salves, although its use should be approached with caution due to its toxicity and potential side effects.
